preamble: The voiceless alveolar plosive /t/ can undergo a phonetic transformation into the voiceless alveolar lateral fricative /ɬ/. This shift involves a change in the manner of articulation from a complete stop to a fricative, and a change in laterality from central to lateral, where air escapes over the sides of the tongue.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (plosive to fricative), change in laterality (central to lateral)

examples.note: Attested from Proto-Joola *t to /ɬ/ in Gubaare. Specific word examples are not provided in the source, but the shift is explicitly documented.

sources: Merrill, J. T. M. (2022). Voiceless rhotic / retroflex consonants as an areal feature of the Atlantic Languages. Language in Africa, 3(2), 45-64.
